/broadcasts.description:
Broadcasts let you fan out an announcement to every relevant user or topic. Use these APIs to create announcements, inspect their status, and audit how they generated notifications and deliveries across your project.

Related guides: [Broadcast Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/broadcast)

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of broadcasts for the project. Returns basic information about each broadcast including its creation time and status.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

post.description: Creates a new broadcast. When a broadcast is created, it generates individual notifications for relevant users within the project.

/broadcasts/{broadcast_id}.description: Endpoints scoped to one broadcast instance. Use them to inspect configuration, track execution timestamps, and debug why a send behaved a certain way.

get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific broadcast by its ID. Includes the broadcast's configuration and current status.

/channels.description:
Channels represent the mediums such as Email, Push, SMS, Slack, through which deliveries happen. These routes configure how each channel behaves inside a project, ensuring every notification honors user preferences and branding.

Related guides: [Channel Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/channel)

put.description: Save the channels configuration for a given key.

get.description: Lists all Inbox tokens belonging to the authenticated user. Returns a paginated list of tokens, including their status, creation dates, and associated metadata.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

put.description: Saves the Inbox token for the authenticated user. This token serves as a credential for accessing channel-specific functionality. Each token is unique to the user and channel combination, allowing for direct communication with the user via the channel.

get.description: Fetches details of a specific Inbox token belonging to the authenticated user. Returns information about the token's status, creation date, and any associated metadata. Users can only access their own tokens.

delete.description: Deletes one of the authenticated user's Inbox tokens. This permanently invalidates the specified token, preventing it from being used for future channel access. This action cannot be undone. Users can only revoke their own tokens.

get.description: Lists all APNs tokens belonging to the authenticated user. Returns a paginated list of tokens, including their status, creation dates, and associated metadata.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

put.description: Saves the APNs token for the authenticated user. This token serves as a credential for accessing channel-specific functionality. Each token is unique to the user and channel combination, allowing for direct communication with the user via the channel.

delete.description: Deletes one of the authenticated user's APNs tokens. This permanently invalidates the specified token, preventing it from being used for future channel access. This action cannot be undone. Users can only revoke their own tokens.

parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

/channels/slack/tokens.description: Tokens tie a user to their Slack destination—devices, inboxes, or workspaces. Register them at login, list them for debugging, and revoke stale entries to keep delivery healthy.

get.description: Lists all Slack tokens belonging to the authenticated user. Returns a paginated list of tokens, including their status, creation dates, and associated metadata.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

put.description: Saves the Slack token for the authenticated user. This token serves as a credential for accessing channel-specific functionality. Each token is unique to the user and channel combination, allowing for direct communication with the user via the channel.

/channels/slack/tokens/{token_id}.description: Tokens tie a user to their Slack destination—devices, inboxes, or workspaces. Register them at login, list them for debugging, and revoke stale entries to keep delivery healthy.

get.description: Fetches details of a specific Slack token belonging to the authenticated user. Returns information about the token's status, creation date, and any associated metadata. Users can only access their own tokens.

delete.description: Deletes one of the authenticated user's Slack tokens. This permanently invalidates the specified token, preventing it from being used for future channel access. This action cannot be undone. Users can only revoke their own tokens.

parameters.name: starting_after

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

put.description: Saves the Teams token for the authenticated user. This token serves as a credential for accessing channel-specific functionality. Each token is unique to the user and channel combination, allowing for direct communication with the user via the channel.

delete.description: Deletes one of the authenticated user's Teams tokens. This permanently invalidates the specified token, preventing it from being used for future channel access. This action cannot be undone. Users can only revoke their own tokens.

/channels/user_preferences.description: Manages each user's opt-outs and per-channel overrides so every notification respects personal choices across devices and mediums.

get.description: Fetch a user's channel delivery preferences.

put.description: Save a user's channel preferences.

/channels/web_push/tokens.description: Tokens tie a user to their Web Push destination—devices, inboxes, or workspaces. Register them at login, list them for debugging, and revoke stale entries to keep delivery healthy.

get.description: Lists all Web Push tokens belonging to the authenticated user. Returns a paginated list of tokens, including their status, creation dates, and associated metadata.

parameters.name: limit

parameters.name: starting_after

/channels/web_push/tokens/{token_id}.description: Tokens tie a user to their Web Push destination—devices, inboxes, or workspaces. Register them at login, list them for debugging, and revoke stale entries to keep delivery healthy.

delete.description: Deletes one of the authenticated user's Web Push tokens. This permanently invalidates the specified token, preventing it from being used for future channel access. This action cannot be undone. Users can only revoke their own tokens.

/channels/{key}.description:
Channels represent the mediums such as Email, Push, SMS, Slack, through which deliveries happen. These routes configure how each channel behaves inside a project, ensuring every notification honors user preferences and branding.

Related guides: [Channel Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/channel)

get.description: Fetches the channels config for a given key.

/events.description:
Events capture everything that happens across deliveries. Query this stream to audit what occurred, troubleshoot delivery issues, and power observability dashboards.

Related guides: [Delivery Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/delivery)

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of events for the project.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

/events/{event_id}.description:
Events capture everything that happens across deliveries. Query this stream to audit what occurred, troubleshoot delivery issues, and power observability dashboards.

Related guides: [Delivery Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/delivery)

get.description: Fetches a project event by its ID.

/integrations.description:
Integrations connect MagicBell to external providers for delivery, data ingestion, and automation. Use these grouped APIs to configure providers, run installation flows, and receive incoming events.

Related guides: [Integration Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/integration)

get.description: Lists all available and configured integrations for the project. Returns a summary of each integration including its type, status, and basic configuration information.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

/integrations/apns.description: Upload APNs credentials to deliver native iOS, iPadOS, and macOS push notifications.

get.description: Retrieves the current APNs integration configurations for a specific integration type in the project. Returns configuration details and status information.

put.description: Updates or creates the APNs integration for the project.

delete.description: Deletes the APNs integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the unique identifier of the integration to be deleted

/integrations/eventsource.description: Generic integration for piping arbitrary event feeds into MagicBell when no dedicated provider exists.

get.description: Retrieves the current EventSource integration configurations for a specific integration type in the project. Returns configuration details and status information.

put.description: Updates or creates the EventSource integration for the project.

delete.description: Deletes the EventSource integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the unique identifier of the integration to be deleted

/integrations/expo.description: Send Expo push notifications to React Native apps with zero extra plumbing.

get.description: Retrieves the current Expo integration configurations for a specific integration type in the project. Returns configuration details and status information.

put.description: Updates or creates the Expo integration for the project.

delete.description: Deletes the Expo integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the unique identifier of the integration to be deleted

/integrations/fcm.description: Connect FCM to reach Android devices and browsers that rely on Google's messaging infrastructure.

get.description: Retrieves the current FCM integration configurations for a specific integration type in the project. Returns configuration details and status information.

put.description: Updates or creates the FCM integration for the project.

delete.description: Deletes the FCM integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the unique identifier of the integration to be deleted

/integrations/github.description: Subscribe to issues, pull requests, and deployments to keep teams informed inside MagicBell.

get.description: Retrieves the current GitHub integration configurations for a specific integration type in the project. Returns configuration details and status information.

put.description: Updates or creates the GitHub integration for the project.

delete.description: Deletes the GitHub integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the unique identifier of the integration to be deleted

/integrations/inbox.description: Manage integrations that sync data into the MagicBell Inbox surfaces, including hosted widgets and embeddable inboxes.

get.description: Retrieves the current Inbox integration configurations for a specific integration type in the project. Returns configuration details and status information.

put.description: Updates or creates the Inbox integration for the project.

delete.description: Deletes the Inbox integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the unique identifier of the integration to be deleted

/integrations/inbox/installations.description: High-level entry point for integrations that require multi-step authorization.

put.description: Creates a new installation of a Inbox integration for a user. This endpoint is used when an integration needs to be set up with user-specific credentials or configuration.

/integrations/inbox/installations/start.description: Begins the OAuth or credentials handshake for an integration, returning URLs or payloads needed to obtain user consent.

post.description: Initiates the installation flow for an Inbox integration. This is the first step in a multi-step installation process where user authorization or external service configuration may be required.

/integrations/magicbell_slackbot.description: Provision the hosted Slackbot that delivers actionable notifications into Slack without you maintaining an app. Ideal when you want a turnkey shared Slack experience.

get.description: Retrieves the current MagicBell SlackBot integration configurations for a specific integration type in the project. Returns configuration details and status information.

put.description: Updates or creates the MagicBell SlackBot integration for the project.

delete.description: Deletes the MagicBell SlackBot integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the unique identifier of the integration to be deleted

/integrations/magicbell_slackbot/install.description: High-level entry point for integrations that require multi-step authorization.

put.description: Creates a new installation of a MagicBell SlackBot integration for a user. This endpoint is used when an integration needs to be set up with user-specific credentials or configuration.

/integrations/magicbell_slackbot/install.description: Completes the installation handshake after the user authorizes access, persisting tokens and validating the external connection.

post.description: Completes the installation flow for the MagicBell SlackBot integration. This endpoint is typically called after the user has completed any required authorization steps with MagicBell SlackBot.

/integrations/magicbell_slackbot/install.description: Begins the OAuth or credentials handshake for an integration, returning URLs or payloads needed to obtain user consent.

post.description: Initiates the installation flow for a MagicBell SlackBot integration. This is the first step in a multi-step installation process where user authorization or external service configuration may be required.

put.description: Updates or creates the Mailgun integration for the project.

delete.description: Deletes the Mailgun integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the unique identifier of the integration to be deleted

delete.description: Deletes the SendGrid integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

delete.description: Deletes the Amazon SES integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

/integrations/slack.description: Configure Slack so broadcasts can post rich updates into channels or DMs. The flow covers OAuth installation, workspace selection, and webhook delivery setup.

delete.description: Deletes the Slack integration configuration from the project. This will disable the integration's functionality within the project.

parameters.description: the unique identifier of the integration to be deleted

/integrations/slack/installations.description: High-level entry point for integrations that require multi-step authorization.

put.description: Creates a new installation of a Slack integration for a user. This endpoint is used when an integration needs to be set up with user-specific credentials or configuration.

/integrations/slack/installations/finish.description: Completes the installation handshake after the user authorizes access, persisting tokens and validating the external connection.

post.description: Completes the installation flow for the Slack integration. This endpoint is typically called after the user has completed any required authorization steps with Slack.

/integrations/slack/installations/start.description: Begins the OAuth or credentials handshake for an integration, returning URLs or payloads needed to obtain user consent.

post.description: Initiates the installation flow for a Slack integration. This is the first step in a multi-step installation process where user authorization or external service configuration may be required.

parameters.description: the unique identifier of the integration to be deleted

/integrations/web_push/installations/sta.description: Begins the OAuth or credentials handshake for an integration, returning URLs or payloads needed to obtain user consent.

post.description: Initiates the installation flow for a Web Push integration. This is the first step in a multi-step installation process where user authorization or external service configuration may be required.

/notifications.description:
Notifications are the per-recipient records created from broadcasts. These endpoints let a user list, fetch, and manage the notifications that drive their inbox experience.

Related guides: [Notification Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/notification)

get.description: Lists all notifications for a user.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: filter notifications by their read state, one of 'unread' | 'read' | 'archived'

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: filter notifications by their category

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: filter notifications by their topic

/notifications/archive.description:
Notifications are the per-recipient records created from broadcasts. These endpoints let a user list, fetch, and manage the notifications that drive their inbox experience.

Related guides: [Notification Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/notification)

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: filter notifications by their category

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: filter notifications by their topic

/notifications/read.description:
Notifications are the per-recipient records created from broadcasts. These endpoints let a user list, fetch, and manage the notifications that drive their inbox experience.

Related guides: [Notification Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/notification)

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: filter notifications by their category

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: filter notifications by their topic

/notifications/unread/count.description:
Notifications are the per-recipient records created from broadcasts. These endpoints let a user list, fetch, and manage the notifications that drive their inbox experience.

Related guides: [Notification Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/notification)

get.description: Returns the count of unread notifications for a user. Supports filtering by category and topic.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: filter notifications by their category

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: filter notifications by their topic

/notifications/{notification_id}.description:
Notifications are the per-recipient records created from broadcasts. These endpoints let a user list, fetch, and manage the notifications that drive their inbox experience.

Related guides: [Notification Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/notification)

get.description: Gets a notification by ID.

/notifications/{notification_id}/archive.description:
Notifications are the per-recipient records created from broadcasts. These endpoints let a user list, fetch, and manage the notifications that drive their inbox experience.

Related guides: [Notification Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/notification)

/notifications/{notification_id}/read.description:
Notifications are the per-recipient records created from broadcasts. These endpoints let a user list, fetch, and manage the notifications that drive their inbox experience.

Related guides: [Notification Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/notification)

Notifications are the per-recipient records created from broadcasts. These endpoints let a user list, fetch, and manage the notifications that drive their inbox experience.

Related guides: [Notification Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/notification)

Notifications are the per-recipient records created from broadcasts. These endpoints let a user list, fetch, and manage the notifications that drive their inbox experience.

Related guides: [Notification Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/notification)

/users.description:
Users are the recipients of every notification. These endpoints let you list users, upsert profiles, and remove users while keeping identifiers aligned with your product database.

Related guides: [User Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/user)

get.description: Lists all users in the project.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: filter users by their email address or external ID

put.description: Creates or updates a user with the provided details. The user will be associated with the project specified in the request context.

/users/{user_id}.description:
Users are the recipients of every notification. These endpoints let you list users, upsert profiles, and remove users while keeping identifiers aligned with your product database.

Related guides: [User Primitive](https://www.magicbell.com/docs/primitive/user)

delete.description: Removes a user and all associated data from the project.

get.description: Lists all Inbox tokens associated with a specific user. This endpoint is available to project administrators and returns a paginated list of tokens, including both active and revoked tokens.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

get.description: Fetches a specific Inbox token by its ID for a given user. This endpoint is available to project administrators and requires project-level authentication. Use this to inspect token details including its status, creation date, and associated metadata.

delete.description: Deletes a specific user's Inbox token. This endpoint is available to project administrators and permanently invalidates the specified token. Once revoked, the token can no longer be used to access channel features. This action cannot be undone.

get.description: Lists all APNs tokens associated with a specific user. This endpoint is available to project administrators and returns a paginated list of tokens, including both active and revoked tokens.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

get.description: Fetches a specific APNs token by its ID for a given user. This endpoint is available to project administrators and requires project-level authentication. Use this to inspect token details including its status, creation date, and associated metadata.

delete.description: Deletes a specific user's APNs token. This endpoint is available to project administrators and permanently invalidates the specified token. Once revoked, the token can no longer be used to access channel features. This action cannot be undone.

get.description: Lists all Expo tokens associated with a specific user. This endpoint is available to project administrators and returns a paginated list of tokens, including both active and revoked tokens.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: defines the maximum number of items to return per page (default: 50)

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the last ID in previous page

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: a cursor for use in pagination, points to the first ID in next page

/workflows.description: Workflows let you orchestrate multi-step reactions to events, fan out to broadcasts, enrich data, or call external services. These routes manage definitions and runs so you can iterate on automation safely.

get.description: Retrieves all workflow definitions for the project

put.description: Creates or updates a workflow definition for the project

/workflows/*.description: Workflows let you orchestrate multi-step reactions to events, fan out to broadcasts, enrich data, or call external services. These routes manage definitions and runs so you can iterate on automation safely.

get.description: Retrieves a workflow definition by key

/workflows/runs.description: Workflows let you orchestrate multi-step reactions to events, fan out to broadcasts, enrich data, or call external services. These routes manage definitions and runs so you can iterate on automation safely.

post.description: Executes a workflow with the provided input parameters

/workflows/runs/{run_id}.description: Workflows let you orchestrate multi-step reactions to events, fan out to broadcasts, enrich data, or call external services. These routes manage definitions and runs so you can iterate on automation safely.

get.description: Retrieves the status and details of a workflow run

/workflows/{workflow_key}/runs.description: Workflows let you orchestrate multi-step reactions to events, fan out to broadcasts, enrich data, or call external services. These routes manage definitions and runs so you can iterate on automation safely.

get.description: Retrieves all runs for a specific workflow

app_id.description: The default bundle identifier of the application that is configured with this project. It can be overriden on a per token basis, when registering device tokens.

badge.description: Controls whether the app icon badge counts unread or unseen notifications.

certificate.description: The APNs certificate in P8 format. Generate it at [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/authkeys/add) with the 'Apple Push Notification service (APNs)' option selected.

key_id.description: The 10-character Key ID from your Apple Developer account used with the P8 certificate.

payload_version.description: Internal payload format version used by MagicBell.

team_id.description: The Apple Developer Team ID that owns the configured key.

app_id.description: The bundle identifier of the application registering this token. Use this to override the default identifier configured on the APNs integration.

created_at.description: The timestamp when the token was created.

device_token.description: The APNs device token to register with MagicBell.

discarded_at.description: The timestamp when the token was discarded, if applicable.

id.description: The unique identifier for the token.

installation_id.description: The APNs environment this token belongs to. If omitted we assume it targets `production`.

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the token metadata last changed.

app_id.description: The bundle identifier of the application registering this token. Use this to override the default identifier configured on the APNs integration.

device_token.description: The APNs device token to register with MagicBell.

installation_id.description: The APNs environment this token belongs to. If omitted we assume it targets `production`.

action_url.description: The URL recipients will be directed to when interacting with the broadcast.

category.description: The label used to group broadcasts.

content.description: The body content delivered with the broadcast.

created_at.description: The timestamp when the broadcast was created.

custom_attributes.description: Arbitrary custom data associated with the broadcast.

id.description: The unique id for this broadcast.

overrides.description: Channel- or provider-specific values that override the defaults.

channels.description: Overrides that are scoped to individual delivery channels.

action_url.description: The link associated with the channel-specific notification.
The link associated with the channel-specific notification.
The link associated with the channel-specific notification.
The link associated with the channel-specific notification.

providers.description: Overrides that are scoped to specific providers for a channel.

apns.description: Provider-specific overrides for Apple Push Notification service.

expo.description: Provider-specific overrides for Expo push notifications.

fcm.description: Provider-specific overrides for Firebase Cloud Messaging.

web_push.description: Provider-specific overrides for the web push provider.

recipients.description: A collection of users or filters that determine who receives the broadcast.

items.description: The recipient specification for the broadcast.

status.description: The runtime state of the broadcast execution.

errors.description: A list of errors encountered while processing the broadcast.

message.description: The details about the processing error.

status.description: The overall processing status of the broadcast.

summary.description: The summary counts for total recipients and failures.

failures.description: The number of failures while processing the broadcast.

total.description: The number of recipients that the broadcast was sent to.

title.description: The subject or headline that will be shown to recipients.

topic.description: The topic that further classifies the broadcast.

channels.description: Ordered channel steps the delivery planner should execute.

channel.description: Name of the channel used for this step.

delay.description: Delay in seconds to wait after the previous step.

if.description: Conditional expression evaluated before running the step.

disabled.description: Disables the plan so it cannot be executed.

key.description: Unique identifier for this delivery plan.

count.description: The count of notifications matching the query.

id.description: Identifier of the workflow run that was created.

discarded_at.description: The timestamp when the token was discarded.

id.description: The unique identifier for the discarded token.

code.description: The numeric code that categorizes the event.

context.description: Additional contextual attributes for the event.

id.description: The unique identifier for the event.

level.description: The severity level assigned to the event.

payload.description: The raw payload delivered by the event source.

timestamp.description: The time at which the event was recorded.

type.description: The type of event that occurred.

input.description: Optional JSON payload that will be passed as the workflow input context.

key.description: The unique workflow key to execute (e.g. integration.stripe.charge.succeeded).

private_key.description: The PEM encoded service account private key used to sign Firebase credentials.
